tasks.name: Setup unified training codebase with Phi modulator interface
tasks.description: Create the unified PyTorch training codebase. Implement the WDModulator base class and all method-specific subclasses (AdamW constant, CWD hard/soft, SWD, Cosine-WD, Linear-WD, InverseSqrt-WD, AdamWN, AlphaDecay, CWD+Cosine, CWD+AdamWN, RandomMask, InvertedMask, EffectiveLambdaMatched). Implement diagnostic logging hooks for per-layer weight norms, gradient-weight alignment cosines, effective LR, and Phi values. Implement CSI/AIS/BEM metric computation. Fork data loading and LR scheduling from why-weight-decay (D'Angelo et al. NeurIPS 2024) conventions.

pilot.pass_criteria: All 14 Phi modulators instantiate and produce correct-shaped output on dummy tensors; unit tests pass for CSI/AIS/BEM computation

tasks.name: Prepare CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet data pipelines
tasks.description: Implement data loading for CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100 (torchvision auto-download) and ImageNet-1K (assuming pre-existing on disk or download to shared path). Standard augmentation: RandomCrop+Pad+HFlip for CIFAR; RandAugment+MixUp+CutMix for ImageNet. Verify data loaders produce correct shapes and label distributions.

pilot.pass_criteria: All three datasets load correctly; CIFAR: 50k train / 10k test; ImageNet: 1.28M train / 50k val

tasks.name: Implement model architectures (ResNet-20, VGG-16-BN, ResNet-50, ViT-S)
tasks.description: Implement or import ResNet-20 (CIFAR-scale), VGG-16-BN (CIFAR-scale), ResNet-50 (ImageNet-scale), and ViT-Small/16 (optional, Phase 4). Ensure all models expose per-layer parameter groups for the Phi modulator interface. Add hooks for extracting intermediate layer statistics.

pilot.pass_criteria: All models forward pass on correct input shapes; parameter counts match expected (ResNet-20: ~0.27M, VGG-16-BN: ~15M, ResNet-50: ~25.6M, ViT-S: ~22M)

tasks.name: Implement visualization toolkit (6-panel diagnostics + paper figures)
tasks.description: Create plotting utilities for: (1) training loss + test accuracy curves, (2) per-layer weight norm trajectories, (3) per-layer gradient-weight cosine similarity, (4) effective LR per layer, (5) CSI/AIS evolution, (6) Phi modulation heatmaps. Also create paper figure generators for budget equivalence panels, CWD falsification results, metric scatter plots, and composition analysis. All plots publication-quality (matplotlib + seaborn, proper font sizes, colorblind-safe palettes).

visualizations.description: 6-panel per-method diagnostic
Phi-framework taxonomy diagram

pilot.pass_criteria: All 6 diagnostic panel types render without error on dummy data; output PNG files are non-empty

tasks.name: Pilot: AdamW constant WD baseline on CIFAR-10/ResNet-20
tasks.description: Train ResNet-20 on CIFAR-10 with standard AdamW (constant WD=5e-4, LR=1e-3, cosine LR schedule). 100 epochs, seed 42. Validate that the codebase produces reasonable results (target: >91% test accuracy). Log all diagnostic metrics (weight norms, alignment cosines, effective LR). This serves as the reference point for all subsequent experiments.

visualizations.description: Training loss and test accuracy curves
Per-layer weight norm trajectory

pilot.pass_criteria: Test accuracy > 91% at epoch 100; training loss decreasing monotonically after epoch 10; no NaN/Inf in diagnostics

tasks.name: Pilot: CWD on CIFAR-10/ResNet-20
tasks.description: Train ResNet-20 on CIFAR-10 with CWD-AdamW (hard binary mask, WD=5e-4, LR=1e-3). 100 epochs, seed 42. Log mask ratio per layer, effective lambda, and all diagnostics. Compare against pilot_baseline.

visualizations.description: CWD mask ratio per layer over training
Effective lambda comparison: CWD vs. AdamW

pilot.pass_criteria: CWD trains without error; mask ratio ~ 0.4-0.6; accuracy within ±1% of baseline

tasks.name: Pilot: SWD on CIFAR-10/ResNet-20
tasks.description: Train ResNet-20 on CIFAR-10 with SWD/AdamS (gradient-norm-based WD scaling, sensitivity=1.0, WD=5e-4, LR=1e-3). 100 epochs, seed 42. Log WD multiplier trajectory and all diagnostics.

visualizations.description: SWD multiplier h(||g||) over training

pilot.pass_criteria: SWD trains without error; WD multiplier varies meaningfully (CV > 0.1); accuracy within ±1% of baseline

tasks.name: Pilot: Cosine WD schedule on CIFAR-10/ResNet-20
tasks.description: Train ResNet-20 on CIFAR-10 with cosine WD schedule (WD decays from 5e-4 to 0 following cosine curve, LR=1e-3). 100 epochs, seed 42. Compute mean WD for budget equivalence comparison.

visualizations.description: Cosine WD schedule vs constant WD trajectory

pilot.pass_criteria: Cosine WD trains without error; accuracy within ±1.5% of baseline; mean WD computable

tasks.name: Pilot: Compute and validate CSI/AIS/BEM metrics from pilot runs
tasks.description: From the 4 pilot runs (AdamW, CWD, SWD, Cosine-WD), compute CSI, AIS, and BEM for each method. Verify that: (a) CSI differentiates methods (not all identical), (b) AIS values are computable and meaningful, (c) BEM is near zero for budget-equivalent comparisons. This is the kill-or-continue gate for the metrics component.

visualizations.description: Pilot metrics comparison (CSI, AIS, BEM per method)
CSI vs. accuracy for 4 methods

pilot.pass_criteria: CSI values differ by >10% across methods; AIS computable without NaN; BEM < 0.01 for cosine-WD vs mean-matched constant

tasks.name: Pilot: Soft CWD proximal approximation verification (H1)
tasks.description: Train ResNet-20 on CIFAR-10 with soft CWD at beta ∈ {10, 50, 100, 500, 1000} and compare against hard CWD. 100 epochs, seed 42 each. Verify convergence of soft to hard CWD. This validates the proximal-theoretic unification claim for the CWD case.
tasks.comment: 5 beta values × 100 epochs each, ~10 min per run sequentially on 1 GPU

visualizations.description: Soft CWD accuracy vs beta (convergence to hard CWD)
Mask similarity between soft(beta) and hard CWD over training

pilot.pass_criteria: |acc_hard - acc_soft(beta=100)| < 0.2%; mask similarity > 0.95 for beta >= 100

tasks.name: CIFAR-10/ResNet-20: All 7 primary methods + CWD ablations, 3 seeds
tasks.description: Full CIFAR-10 experiment on ResNet-20 with 200 epochs. Methods: AdamW, SGD+WD, CWD, SWD, Cosine-WD, AdamWN, AlphaDecay. CWD ablations: effective-lambda-matched, random-mask, inverted-mask, continuous-cosine-WD. Total: 11 configs × 3 seeds = 33 runs. HP-tune on seed 42, apply best HP to all seeds. Log all diagnostics every 100 steps.
tasks.comment: 33 runs × ~10 min each = 330 GPU-min; parallelized across 8 GPUs = ~42 min wall + overhead. Each individual run uses 1 GPU; 8 runs execute in parallel.

visualizations.description: CIFAR-10/ResNet-20 results: all methods mean±std
CWD falsification: CWD vs 4 controls
Budget equivalence: temporal schedules vs mean-matched constant

pilot.pass_criteria: AdamW baseline > 92% at 200 epochs; all methods train without error; std across seeds < 0.5%

tasks.name: CIFAR-100/ResNet-20: All 7 primary methods + CWD ablations, 3 seeds
tasks.description: Full CIFAR-100 experiment on ResNet-20 with 200 epochs. Same method set and protocol as cifar10_resnet20_full. CIFAR-100 is the critical second benchmark for cross-dataset validation of budget equivalence and CWD falsification.
tasks.comment: Same structure as cifar10_resnet20. 33 runs × ~10 min each, parallelized across 8 GPUs.

visualizations.description: CIFAR-100/ResNet-20 results
CWD falsification on CIFAR-100

pilot.pass_criteria: AdamW baseline > 68% at 200 epochs; all methods converge; CWD mask ratio stable

tasks.name: CIFAR-10/VGG-16-BN: All 7 methods + spatial modulation focus, 3 seeds
tasks.description: Full CIFAR-10 experiment on VGG-16-BN with 200 epochs. All 7 primary methods, 3 seeds. VGG's heterogeneous layer structure (wide variation in layer sizes) is critical for testing spatial modulation superiority (H6). AlphaDecay-style per-layer WD is expected to show more benefit here than on ResNet.
tasks.comment: 7 methods × 3 seeds = 21 runs × ~15 min each = 315 GPU-min; parallelized across 8 GPUs = ~40 min wall + overhead.

visualizations.description: CIFAR-10/VGG-16-BN results
Per-layer Phi modulation comparison across methods on VGG

pilot.pass_criteria: AdamW baseline > 92.5% at 200 epochs; AlphaDecay shows per-layer variation in Phi values

tasks.name: CIFAR-100/VGG-16-BN: All 7 methods, 3 seeds
tasks.description: Full CIFAR-100 experiment on VGG-16-BN with 200 epochs. All 7 primary methods, 3 seeds. Completes the CIFAR grid (2 datasets × 2 architectures).
tasks.comment: 7 methods × 3 seeds = 21 runs × ~15 min each, parallelized across 8 GPUs.

visualizations.description: CIFAR-100/VGG-16-BN results

pilot.pass_criteria: AdamW baseline > 70% at 200 epochs; all methods converge

tasks.name: WD Stability Condition test: warmup duration sweep (H2)
tasks.description: ResNet-20 on CIFAR-10 with AdamW. Fix LR=1e-3. Two WD targets: lambda=5e-4 and lambda=5e-3. WD warmup from 0 to target over K steps, K ∈ {1, 10, 50, 200, 1000}. 3 seeds each. Measure: training loss variance in steps 0-1000, max loss spike magnitude, gradient norm statistics. The stability condition predicts K < K_critical = 1/(eta*lambda) should cause instability. For lambda=5e-3, K_critical = 200, so K ∈ {1, 10, 50} should show elevated variance.
tasks.comment: 2 WD values × 5 K values × 3 seeds = 30 runs × ~5 min each (only 100 epochs needed, most signal in first 1000 steps). 4 GPUs in parallel.

visualizations.description: Loss variance vs warmup K at different WD strengths
Training loss heatmap (K × step) showing spike regions

pilot.pass_criteria: K=1 with WD=5e-3 shows >2x loss variance compared to K=1000; pattern consistent with stability condition prediction

tasks.name: Composition orthogonality test (H7): CWD+Cosine vs CWD+AdamWN
tasks.description: CIFAR-100/ResNet-20, 200 epochs, 3 seeds. Compare: (a) CWD alone, (b) Cosine-WD alone, (c) CWD+Cosine-WD, (d) AdamWN alone, (e) CWD+AdamWN. Tests whether temporal+directional compose beneficially and whether directional+target are redundant.
tasks.comment: 5 configs × 3 seeds = 15 runs × ~10 min each. 4 GPUs in parallel → ~40 min wall. Some results already available from cifar100_resnet20_full (CWD, Cosine, AdamWN alone). Only compositions need new runs: 2 compositions × 3 seeds = 6 new runs.

visualizations.description: Composition results: individual vs composed methods
Composition orthogonality test

pilot.pass_criteria: CWD+Cosine accuracy >= max(CWD, Cosine) - 0.3%; CWD+AdamWN <= max(CWD, AdamWN) + 0.3%

tasks.name: Spatial vs temporal modulation factor analysis (H6)
tasks.description: Dedicated factor analysis separating spatial and temporal WD contributions. On CIFAR-100 with VGG-16-BN (heterogeneous layers): (a) Temporal-only: cosine WD schedule (all layers same), (b) Spatial-only: per-layer WD based on layer norm ratios, constant in time, (c) Spatial+Temporal: per-layer WD with cosine schedule, (d) Effective-LR control: match CWD's effective LR trajectory using direct LR adjustment with constant WD. 3 seeds each.
tasks.comment: 4 configs × 3 seeds = 12 runs × ~15 min each. 4 GPUs in parallel → ~45 min wall. Some configs overlap with cifar100_vgg16_full.

visualizations.description: Factor analysis: spatial vs temporal contributions
Effective LR trajectories per layer for spatial vs temporal methods

pilot.pass_criteria: Spatial-only outperforms temporal-only by >0.3%; effective-LR control reproduces CWD within 0.5%

tasks.name: ImageNet/ResNet-50: Top methods + AdamW baseline, 3 seeds, DDP
tasks.description: ImageNet-1K training with ResNet-50, 90 epochs. Methods: AdamW (constant WD), CWD, best temporal schedule, AdamWN, AlphaDecay, and CWD+Cosine (top performing or most informative from CIFAR phase). 6 methods × 3 seeds = 18 runs. Each run uses 8 GPUs with DDP. Batch size 256 per GPU (2048 effective). Standard ImageNet augmentation (RandAugment, MixUp, CutMix). This is the scale-up validation phase.
tasks.comment: Each 90-epoch ImageNet run on 8x RTX PRO 6000 takes ~4h. 18 runs sequential (can only use 8-GPU DDP one run at a time) = 72h total, but ~9h wall clock if we run 1 config at a time. OVERRIDE: ImageNet requires longer time budget per project constraints.

visualizations.description: ImageNet/ResNet-50 results: top methods
ImageNet training curves comparison
Cross-dataset metric consistency: CIFAR CSI vs ImageNet CSI

pilot.pass_criteria: DDP setup functional on 8 GPUs; AdamW baseline converges to >75% top-1 at epoch 30 (extrapolation); no communication deadlocks

tasks.name: ViT-S/16 on CIFAR-100: Architecture generalization test (H6)
tasks.description: ViT-Small/16 on CIFAR-100, 200 epochs, 4 methods (AdamW, CWD, AlphaDecay, best temporal), 3 seeds. Tests whether Phi framework predictions hold for LayerNorm architectures (vs. BatchNorm in ResNet/VGG). ViT's attention layers have fundamentally different gradient-weight alignment patterns.
tasks.comment: 4 methods × 3 seeds = 12 runs × ~20 min each. 4 GPUs in parallel → ~60 min wall.

visualizations.description: ViT-S/CIFAR-100 results: BN vs LN architecture comparison
Phi modulation patterns in ViT attention layers

pilot.pass_criteria: ViT-S converges to >70% on CIFAR-100 with AdamW; no training instability with CWD on attention layers

tasks.name: Cross-benchmark metric predictiveness analysis (H5)
tasks.description: Aggregate CSI/AIS/BEM from all CIFAR experiments (4 benchmark settings × 7-11 methods × 3 seeds). Compute Spearman rank correlations between metrics and accuracy rankings. Cross-validate: train metric-ranking model on CIFAR-10, predict rankings on CIFAR-100. If ImageNet results available, test transfer from CIFAR to ImageNet.

visualizations.description: CSI vs accuracy across all benchmarks (Spearman rho)
AIS by method category (alignment-aware vs agnostic)
Metric predictiveness: Spearman rho per benchmark

pilot.pass_criteria: Spearman rho(CSI, Acc) > 0.3 on at least 2/4 benchmarks; AIS differentiates CWD from non-alignment methods

tasks.name: Generate all publication-quality figures and tables
tasks.description: Using the visualization toolkit, generate all paper figures from the aggregated experimental results: (1) Phi-framework taxonomy diagram, (2) WD Stability panel, (3) Budget equivalence multi-panel, (4) CWD falsification battery panel, (5) CSI/AIS scatter plots, (6) Spatial vs temporal comparison, (7) Composition test results. Generate all 5 tables. Generate supplementary 6-panel diagnostics for each method on each benchmark.

visualizations.description: 7 main figures + 5 tables + supplementary panels

pilot.pass_criteria: All 7 main figures and 5 tables render as PNG/PDF without error; visual inspection passes (axes labeled, legends present, no truncation)

tasks.name: Comprehensive statistical analysis and results synthesis
tasks.description: Perform all statistical tests: paired t-tests with Bonferroni correction, bootstrap 95% CIs, Cohen's d effect sizes. Synthesize findings per hypothesis: (1) Proximal unification validated? (2) Stability condition predictive? (3) Budget equivalence at scale? (4) CWD mechanism decomposed? (5) Metrics predictive? (6) Spatial > temporal? (7) Composition orthogonality? Write structured results summary with evidence grades for each hypothesis.

visualizations.description: Hypothesis validation summary

pilot.pass_criteria: Statistical tests run without error on pilot data; all 7 hypotheses have a verdict entry; JSON output is well-formed
